Canadian ice hockey player (born 1974)

**Robert Wade Niedermayer Jr.**[[1]](#cite_note-1) (born December 28, 1974) is a Canadian former professional [ice hockey](/wiki/Ice_hockey "Ice hockey") player who played 17 [National Hockey League](/wiki/National_Hockey_League "National Hockey League") (NHL) seasons for the [Florida Panthers](/wiki/Florida_Panthers "Florida Panthers"), [Calgary Flames](/wiki/Calgary_Flames "Calgary Flames"), [Anaheim Ducks](/wiki/Anaheim_Ducks "Anaheim Ducks"), [New Jersey Devils](/wiki/New_Jersey_Devils "New Jersey Devils"), and [Buffalo Sabres](/wiki/Buffalo_Sabres "Buffalo Sabres").

He is the younger brother of former NHL [defenceman](/wiki/Defenceman "Defenceman") [Scott Niedermayer](/wiki/Scott_Niedermayer "Scott Niedermayer") (the two were teammates for four seasons).[[2]](#cite_note-McEvoy-2) Born in [Cassiar, British Columbia](/wiki/Cassiar,_British_Columbia "Cassiar, British Columbia"), he and his brother Scott grew up in [Cranbrook](/wiki/Cranbrook,_British_Columbia "Cranbrook, British Columbia").

## Playing career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rob_Niedermayer&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Playing career")]

Niedermayer was drafted fifth overall by the [Florida Panthers](/wiki/Florida_Panthers "Florida Panthers") in the [1993 NHL entry draft](/wiki/1993_NHL_entry_draft "1993 NHL entry draft"). At the time, he was playing for the [Medicine Hat Tigers](/wiki/Medicine_Hat_Tigers "Medicine Hat Tigers") of the [Western Hockey League](/wiki/Western_Hockey_League "Western Hockey League") (WHL). He began his NHL career immediately following his draft, without playing a game in the minors. During the [1995–96 season](/wiki/1995%E2%80%9396_NHL_season "1995–96 NHL season"), he set career highs in goals, assists and points in the regular season. In the [1996 Stanley Cup playoffs](/wiki/1996_Stanley_Cup_playoffs "1996 Stanley Cup playoffs"), the Panthers went on an improbable playoff drive to the [Stanley Cup Finals](/wiki/1996_Stanley_Cup_Finals "1996 Stanley Cup Finals") before being swept in four games by the [Colorado Avalanche](/wiki/Colorado_Avalanche "Colorado Avalanche"). 1998-99 was his best season, putting up 18 goals and 33 assists for 51 points; however, Niedermayer was hurt by concussions in the next years and was never able to achieve the same level of scoring ability again. His NHL role soon turned into a defensive forward.

On July 23, 2001, Niedermayer was traded to the [Calgary Flames](/wiki/Calgary_Flames "Calgary Flames") (alongside a draft pick) in exchange for [Valeri Bure](/wiki/Valeri_Bure "Valeri Bure") and [Jason Wiemer](/wiki/Jason_Wiemer "Jason Wiemer").[[3]](#cite_note-3) At the 2003 [NHL trade deadline](/wiki/NHL_trade_deadline "NHL trade deadline"), he was sent to the [Mighty Ducks of Anaheim](/wiki/Anaheim_Ducks "Anaheim Ducks").[[4]](#cite_note-4) That same year, he helped the Anaheim Ducks make an [improbable run](/wiki/Cinderella_(sports) "Cinderella (sports)") to the [Stanley Cup Finals](/wiki/2003_Stanley_Cup_Finals "2003 Stanley Cup Finals"), his second such run. But again, Niedermayer's team lost, although this time it was in seven games to his brother's team, the [New Jersey Devils](/wiki/New_Jersey_Devils "New Jersey Devils"). He and Scott became the first set of brothers to play against each other in the Stanley Cup Finals since [Ken](/wiki/Ken_Reardon "Ken Reardon") and [Terry Reardon](/wiki/Terry_Reardon "Terry Reardon") did it in [1946](/wiki/1946_Stanley_Cup_Finals "1946 Stanley Cup Finals").[[2]](#cite_note-McEvoy-2) In the summer of 2005, Rob's brother Scott was signed by the Mighty Ducks, and for the first time in their NHL careers, the two brothers were playing on the same team.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

He and his brother Scott (by this point captain of the Ducks, with Rob as an alternate) won the [Stanley Cup](/wiki/Stanley_Cup "Stanley Cup") together as members of the Anaheim Ducks after defeating the [Ottawa Senators](/wiki/Ottawa_Senators "Ottawa Senators") four games to one in [2007](/wiki/2007_Stanley_Cup_Finals "2007 Stanley Cup Finals"), becoming the first brothers to win the Stanley Cup together since [Duane](/wiki/Duane_Sutter "Duane Sutter") and [Brent Sutter](/wiki/Brent_Sutter "Brent Sutter") accomplished the feat twice with the [New York Islanders](/wiki/New_York_Islanders "New York Islanders") in 1982 and 1983.[[2]](#cite_note-McEvoy-2) After Scott took the first lap around the ice with the Stanley Cup, he handed it to Rob. This was a break with Stanley Cup tradition; normally, the alternate captain who has waited longest to win the Cup (in the 2006–07 Ducks' case, [Chris Pronger](/wiki/Chris_Pronger "Chris Pronger") and [Teemu Selänne](/wiki/Teemu_Sel%C3%A4nne "Teemu Selänne")) takes the next lap after the captain takes his lap.

On September 25, 2009, Niedermayer was signed by the New Jersey Devils, a team his brother Scott played on for 14 NHL seasons. On July 7, 2010, Niedermayer was signed by the [Buffalo Sabres](/wiki/Buffalo_Sabres "Buffalo Sabres") to a one-year contract worth a reported $1.15 million.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/3/33/RNiedermayer1.JPG/250px-RNiedermayer1.JPG)](/wiki/File:RNiedermayer1.JPG)

Rob Niedermayer warming up during the 2007 playoffs

After becoming a free agent following the 2011 season, Niedermayer signed with [HC Lugano](/wiki/HC_Lugano "HC Lugano") of the Swiss [National League A](/wiki/National_League_(ice_hockey) "National League (ice hockey)").

## Personal

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rob_Niedermayer&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Personal")]

On July 16, 2006, Niedermayer married longtime girlfriend Jessica Bentall, daughter of [Barney Bentall](/wiki/Barney_Bentall "Barney Bentall"), whom he had been dating since March 2001. Together, they have three daughters.[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9)

Niedermayer was a minority owner of major junior club the [Kootenay Ice](/wiki/Kootenay_Ice "Kootenay Ice") in the WHL for 18 years before selling his stake (along with brother Scott) to the [Chynoweth](/wiki/Dean_Chynoweth "Dean Chynoweth") family after the [2015–16 season](/wiki/2015%E2%80%9316_WHL_season "2015–16 WHL season").[[10]](#cite_note-10)

## Transactions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rob_Niedermayer&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Transactions")]

* June 26, 1993 – drafted by the [Florida Panthers](/wiki/Florida_Panthers "Florida Panthers") in the first round, fifth overall in the [1993 NHL entry draft](/wiki/1993_NHL_entry_draft "1993 NHL entry draft")
* June 23, 2001 – traded to the [Calgary Flames](/wiki/Calgary_Flames "Calgary Flames") with a 2001 second round draft pick ([Andrei Medvedev](/w/index.php?title=Andrei_Medvedev_(ice_hockey)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Andrei Medvedev (ice hockey) (page does not exist)")) for [Valeri Bure](/wiki/Valeri_Bure "Valeri Bure") and [Jason Wiemer](/wiki/Jason_Wiemer "Jason Wiemer")
* March 11, 2003 – traded to the [Mighty Ducks of Anaheim](/wiki/Anaheim_Ducks "Anaheim Ducks") for [Jean-François Damphousse](/wiki/Jean-Fran%C3%A7ois_Damphousse "Jean-François Damphousse") and [Mike Commodore](/wiki/Mike_Commodore "Mike Commodore")
* September 25, 2009 – signed by the [New Jersey Devils](/wiki/New_Jersey_Devils "New Jersey Devils") as an unrestricted free agent
* July 7, 2010 – signed by the [Buffalo Sabres](/wiki/Buffalo_Sabres "Buffalo Sabres") as an unrestricted free agent
